title: torilis arvensis subsp. recta
intro: Torilis arvensis subsp. recta, commonly known as hemlock parsley or wild chervil, is a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and diuretic effects, which can help alleviate various health issues such as arthritis, digestive problems, and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of this plant include reducing swelling, relieving pain, and promoting relaxation, making it a valuable herbal remedy for overall well-being. Torilis arvensis subsp. recta can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
